articles.title: Markets slide 1% as renewed Iran-Israel tensions lift crude oil prices
articles.description: Benchmark indices post their steepest decline in over two months as rising crude prices, geopolitical uncertainty and global risk aversion dampen investor sentiment

articles.title: Michigan gas prices drop 20 cents a gallon amid Mideast conflict
articles.description: Some fear the renewed strikes between Israel and Iran could threaten to upend a ceasefire and raise fuel prices.

articles.title: Market Wealth Erodes By ₹6.31 Lakh Crore, Sensex Crashes 719 Points As Oil Nears $100 And Global Markets Tumble
articles.description: Indian investors lost Rs 6.31 lakh crore in wealth on Monday as Sensex plunged 719 points and Nifty fell below 23,150. Rising crude oil prices, escalating Iran-Israel tensions, and a sharp global market sell-off triggered broad-based selling across sectors, dragging market sentiment lower.

articles.title: Tech Surge and Geopolitical Ease Give Wall Street a Boost
articles.description: Wall Street rebounded on Monday, led by a surge in chipmaker stocks and eased geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. Key performers included Intel, Nvidia, and Micron, as positive developments drove market optimism despite recent fluctuations. Strong job data and oil prices also influenced trading trends.

articles.title: AI stocks recover some of last week's sell-off, while oil prices come off their overnight highs
articles.description: Wall Street is clawing back some of its steep losses from last week
